title: Debt Payoff Calculator | firemaths.info
description: We ran the math on Debt Snowball vs Avalanche for $50k in balances. The faster payoff strategy isn't what most experts claim. See your exact timeline.

schemas.name: Debt Payoff Calculator
schemas.description: Compare the Snowball and Avalanche debt payoff strategies. Calculate your total interest saved and debt-free date with extra monthly payments.

mainEntity.name: What is a debt payoff calculator?

acceptedAnswer.text: A debt payoff calculator is a tool that helps you determine how long it will take to become debt-free by calculating your payoff timeline based on your current balances, interest rates, and monthly payments.

mainEntity.name: How does a debt snowball calculator work?

acceptedAnswer.text: A debt snowball calculator organizes your debts from smallest balance to largest. You pay the minimum on all debts while applying any extra money to the smallest balance first, giving you quick wins.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between debt avalanche vs snowball?

acceptedAnswer.text: The debt avalanche method targets the debt with the highest interest rate first to save the most money. The debt snowball method targets the lowest balance first for psychological momentum. Both strategies require paying minimums on all other debts.
Mathematically, the debt avalanche strategy will always save you more money on interest and typically results in a faster overall payoff time.

mainEntity.name: How much can extra payments help?

acceptedAnswer.text: Even a small extra monthly payment can significantly reduce your principal balance over time, which means less interest accumulates and can shave years off your payoff timeline.

schemas.headline: Debt Payoff Calculator: Snowball vs Avalanche — Which Saves More?
